'''Joshua Hodgson''' (born 31 October 1989) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who played primarily as a [[hooker (rugby league)|hooker]],<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and has represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at an international level.

He has previously played for [[Hull FC]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] and for the [[Canberra Raiders]] and [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

Hodgson began his senior club career with [[Hull F.C.]] after progressing through their Academy system, making his first team début in 2009. Due to limited first team opportunities, he joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at the end of the 2009 season, where he established himself in the first team and went on to make over 100 appearances. At the end of the 2014 season, he moved to [[Canberra Raiders]].

An England international, Hodgson made his senior international début in October 2014, and represented England at the [[Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] and [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|2017 World Cup]].

Hodgson has suffered three career threatening [[Anterior cruciate ligament injury|Anterior Cruciate Ligament (ACL) injuries]], in 2018, 2020 and 2022, on both knees.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-josh-hodgson-acl-injury-canberra-raiders-jamal-fogarty-tom-starling-parramatta-eels-contract/news-story/fcfb89e80447551bf4e381fd7042e4e6 | title='Window has closed': Why Hodgson injury is a 'concern' for Raiders — and Eels | date=21 March 2022 }}</ref> He suffered a neck injury in 2023 whilst playing for the Parramatta Eels which forced him to retire.

==Background==
Hodgson was born on 31 October 1989 in [[Kingston upon Hull]], England, where he grew up on a housing estate with his mum and step father, one brother, and 5  half-brothers.<ref name="cleary">{{cite web|last1=Cleary|first1=Matt|title=From Hull to the NRL: the remarkable rise of Josh Hodgson|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/apr/08/from-hull-to-the-nrl-the-remarkable-rise-of-josh-hodgson|website=The Guardian|access-date=20 January 2018|date=8 April 2016}}</ref> His step father, Dave, briefly played [[rugby league]] for both [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] before turning to coaching at amateur level.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Crawley|first1=Paul|title=Why Josh Hodgson was so keen to stay with the Canberra Raiders|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/raiders/why-josh-hodgson-was-so-keen-to-stay-with-the-canberra-raiders/news-story/4c331ed6206dd2ed52e61e36d9cac591|website=The Daily Telegraph|access-date=20 January 2018|date=18 March 2016}}</ref>

Hodgson played junior rugby league with [[East Hull A.R.L.F.C.|East Hull]], where he was coached by former Hull player [[Lee Radford]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Bower|first1=Aaron|title=Radford proud of Hull KR star Hodgson's path to England|url=http://www.totalrl.com/radford-proud-hull-kr-star-hodgsons-path-england|website=Total Rugby League|access-date=20 January 2018|date=24 October 2014}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Hull FC===
Hodgson joined Hull as a scholarship player at the age of 13.<ref name="cleary" /> Hodgson represented England at under-17's and 18's level, and progressed through the club's academy ranks before being promoted to the first team for the start of the 2009 season.<ref name="yp">{{cite web|title=Former Leeds Rhino Mark Calderwood joins Hull FC|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/former-leeds-rhino-mark-calderwood-joins-hull-fc-1-2515798|website=Yorkshire Post|access-date=20 January 2018|date=11 September 2008}}</ref> He made his [[Super League]] début in May 2009 against [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Hull Go Down To Warrington|url=http://www.hullfc.com/news/2009-05-16-hull-go-down-to-warrington|publisher=Hull F.C.|access-date=20 January 2018|date=16 May 2009|archive-date=20 January 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180120182245/http://www.hullfc.com/news/2009-05-16-hull-go-down-to-warrington|url-status=dead}}</ref> He played two games during the season as a [[Rugby league positions#The back row|back row forward]],<ref name="cleary" /> which coach [[Richard Agar]] believed to be his best position.<ref name="yp" /> Seeking more regular first team opportunities, Hodgson left Hull at the end of the season to join Hull Kingston Rovers.
[[File:Josh Hodgson.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Hodgson playing for Hull KR in 2014]]

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
Hodgson joined Hull KR on a two-year contract, and was signed as the club's second-choice [[hooker (rugby league)|hooker]] behind [[Ben Fisher]] following the departure of [[Daniel Fitzhenry]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/8234806.stm|title=Hull KR sign Hull hooker Hodgson|date=2 September 2009|work=[[BBC Sport]]|publisher=BBC|access-date=2009-09-09}}</ref> He had an impressive 2012 season, scoring 14 tries and winning the club's player of the year and players' player of the year awards, and was rewarded with a new four-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|title=Hull KR's Josh Hodgson signs four-year contract|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19989609|website=BBC Sport|access-date=20 January 2018|date=18 October 2012}}</ref> Hodgson made his 100th appearance for the club during the 2013 season, and won the players' player of the year award for the second successive year.<ref>{{cite web|title=Dobson crowned Player of the Year|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/dobson-crowned-player-of-the-year|publisher=Hull Kingston Rovers|access-date=20 January 2018|date=5 September 2013}}</ref> The departure of [[Michael Dobson (rugby league)|Michael Dobson]] at the end of the 2013 season meant that Hodgson, along with scrum-half [[Travis Burns (rugby league)|Travis Burns]], were named co-captains for the 2014 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Burns and Hodgson share Rovers captain role|url=https://www.eurosport.com/rugby-league/burns-and-hodgson-share-rovers-captain-role_sto4093954/story.shtml|website=Eurosport|access-date=20 January 2018|date=17 January 2014}}</ref>

===Canberra Raiders===
On 4 August 2014, it was announced that Hodgson had agreed to sign for [[National Rugby League]] side [[Canberra Raiders]] for the 2015 season on a two-year deal, with Canberra paying a "substantial transfer fee" to Hull KR for Hodgson's services.<ref>{{cite web|title=Hull KR profit as Hodgson goes down under|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr/hull-kr-profit-as-hodgson-goes-down-under-1-6766627|website=Yorkshire Post|access-date=20 January 2018|date=4 August 2014}}</ref>

Hodgson made his début against [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in March 2015, and went on to make 24 appearances during the 2015 season, scoring two tries, including a game winning [[golden point]] try against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].

Hodgson enjoyed a breakthrough season in 2016 as he led Canberra to a top-two finish, the first for the club since the [[1995 ARL season]]. Hodgson made 26 appearances during the season, and was considered a front-runner to win the [[Dally M Medal]] for most of the season, but was effectively ruled out after a suspension for a [[grapple tackle]] on [[Melbourne Storm]] prop [[Jesse Bromwich]] cost him three points and a missed game.<ref>{{cite web|title=Josh Hodgson: England hooker misses out on Dally M as Cronk and Taumalolo win|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37495616|website=BBC Sport|access-date=20 January 2018|date=28 September 2016}}</ref> In his first NRL finals appearance, he would inspire the Raiders to a twelve-point lead over the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], however, he would miss out on most of the second half due to an ankle injury. This was the turning point in the match as Cronulla-Sutherland would record a 16-14 win. In the lead up to the semi-final against the [[Penrith Panthers]] much speculation rested on whether Hodgson would play. He was cleared to play and was a standout in the 22-12 triumph. The season would end in Melbourne the following week in a close 14-12 loss. Hodgson was selected by Wayne Bennett for the England Four Nations team.

Hodgson missed the first 14 games of the [[2018 NRL season]] with a knee injury and made his first appearance of the year in Canberra's 48-12 victory over the [[Wests Tigers]] in round 15.<ref>{{cite web|last=Zemek |first=Steve |url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/2018/06/17/18/25/hodgson-lifts-raiders-in-rout-of-tigers |title=NRL News: Josh Hodgson powers Canberra Raiders to victory of Wests Tigers |publisher=Wwos.nine.com.au |date=2018-06-17 |access-date=2018-07-23}}</ref>

He joined the leadership team by being appointed as the co-captain for the Canberra.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2019-canberra-raiders-co-captains-jarrod-croker-josh-hodgson/eygz60gvtkgp108iya02hmu99|title=Canberra Raiders announce Josh Hodgson and Jarrod Croker as co-captains for 2019|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=20 December 2018 |language=en-au|access-date=2019-12-28}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/oct/02/josh-hodgson-travels-with-raiders-to-sydney-and-will-play-nrl-grand-final|title=Josh Hodgson travels with Raiders to Sydney and will play NRL grand final|agency=Australian Associated Press|date=2019-10-02|work=The Guardian|access-date=2019-12-28|language=en-GB|issn=0261-3077}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/why-canberra-raiders-aidan-sezer-is-perfect-addition-for-huddersfield-giants-1-10127890|title=Why Canberra Raiders' Aidan Sezer is perfect addition for Huddersfield Giants|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk|language=en|access-date=2019-12-28}}</ref> Hodgson made a total of 24 appearances for Canberra in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club reached their first grand final in 25 years.  Hodgson played in the club's [[2019 NRL Grand Final]] defeat against the [[Sydney Roosters]] at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings-roosters-and-raiders-hits-and-misses-20191006-p52y53.html/|title=NRL grand final player ratings: Roosters and Raiders hits and misses|website=SMH|date=6 October 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49950757|title=Sydney Roosters beat Canberra Raiders to win NRL Grand Final|work=BBC Sport |date=6 October 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/grand-final-ricky-stuart-reaction-canberra-raiders-six-again-jack-wighton/ae39b639-d375-4608-89a6-af6285c3d635/|title=How Ricky Stuart reacted to grand final 'six-again' refereeing shocker|website=WWOS|date=6 October 2019 }}</ref>

In the [[2020 NRL season]], Hodgson made nine appearances as Canberra finished 5th and qualified for the finals.  He missed the club's finals campaign due to injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/ricky-stuart-storms-out-of-canberra-raiders-press-conference-after-one-question/edab3d4d-0eeb-4960-acc0-89d06797f8e0/|title=Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart storms out of press conference after one question|website=wwos.nine.com.au|date=16 October 2020 }}</ref>

Hodgson made 20 appearances for Canberra in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished a disappointing 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-canberra-raiders-vs-sydney-roosters-live-blog-live-stream-supercoach-scores-videos-jack-wighton-james-tedesco/news-story/745b280dcc746da257fecd8fda231afd/|title=Roosters' 'Mr Fix It' stuns with hat-trick, Sloppy Raiders' final fade out ends season: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=2 September 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2021-roosters-v-canberra-adam-keighran-stars-as-raiders-season-ends-with-shocking-defeat/news-story/f9ef95652ed66e97e4e047245f11da7b/|title=Tough conversations coming for senior Canberra stars as interest grows in Parramatta young gun Dylan Brown|website=www.theaustralian.com.au}}</ref>
On 10 December 2021, Hodgson signed a two-year deal to join [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] starting in the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/12/10/hodgson-simonsson-ink-deals-with-eels/|title=Hodgson, Simonsson ink deals with Eels|website=www.nrl.com|date=9 December 2021 }}</ref>
In round 1 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Hodgson was taken from the field during Canberra's victory over Cronulla with a suspected knee injury.   The following week, Hodgson was ruled out of the entire [[2022 NRL season]] with an ACL injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-josh-hodgson-acl-injury-canberra-raiders-jamal-fogarty-tom-starling-parramatta-eels-contract/news-story/fcfb89e80447551bf4e381fd7042e4e6/|title='Window has closed': Why Hodgson injury is a 'concern' for Raiders — and Eels|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=21 March 2022 }}</ref>

===Parramatta Eels===
Hodgson made his club debut for Parramatta in round 1 of the [[2023 NRL season]] against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]]. Parramatta would lose 16-12 in golden point extra-time.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2023/mar/02/nrl-live-parramatta-vs-melbourne-eels-v-storm-2023-season-opener-live-score-updates-rugby-league/|title=Melbourne Storm beat Parramatta Eels in golden point: NRL 2023 season opener – as it happened|newspaper=The Guardian|date=2 March 2023 |last1=Howcroft |first1=Jonathan }}</ref>
On 30 August 2023, Hodgson announced his retirement from rugby league after suffering a neck injury in round 13 of the competition. Hodgson announced via his social media account he was retiring writing “This is never the way I thought I’d be announcing the end of my footy career, but unfortunately I have been forced to medically retire".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-josh-hodgson-medically-retired-parramatta-eels-neck-injury-canberra-raiders-hull/news-story/19fae6768e3bf8cfc78e657467175613/|title='Never the way I thought it would end': NRL veteran Josh Hodgson medically retired|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=30 August 2023 }}</ref>

==International career==
In 2012, Hodgson was called up to the [[England Knights]] squad to play [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], scoring a try in a 62–4 win. In October 2013, he was named captain of England Knights while being first standby for the senior squad.

In 2014, Hodgson was selected for the [[England national rugby league team|England squad]] for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]] in Australia. He made his début in the opening game against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]], and also played in the second match against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]], but was dropped for the final game against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]. Hodgson was disciplined by England after he was filmed smashing through a door at a student flat in [[Dunedin]] following the New Zealand game,<ref>{{cite web|title=Josh Hodgson: England hooker faces action over party video|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29975929|website=BBC Sport|access-date=20 January 2018|date=9 November 2014}}</ref> an incident which he later admitted he was embarrassed about and was out-of-character.<ref>{{cite web|title=English hooker Hodgson embarrassed, angry|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/10732284/English-hooker-Hodgson-embarrassed-angry|website=Stuff.co.nz|publisher=Fairfax New Zealand|access-date=20 January 2018|date=12 November 2014}}</ref>

In 2015, Hodgson was named in the 24-man England team that played [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in a [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|three match test-series]] held in England. Beforehand England took on France in a test match held in Leigh. Josh scored a try in England's try scoring rout over their opponents.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live/match/43791/report|title=England demolish France 84-4 in record win|publisher=Skysports.com|access-date=24 October 2015}}</ref> He scored his second test try a week later in England's 26-12 win over New Zealand in the first Baskerville Shield test. 
In 2016, Hodgson was named in the 24-man England team that will play in the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]]. Before the tournament began, he featured in a [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations#France vs England|test match]] against France.

In 2017, Hodgson was included in England's [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] squad,<ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|publisher=Mirror|date=9 October 2017|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref> helping his team reach the final against Australia, but did not play in the final itself after suffering a serious knee injury in the semi-final victory over [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]].<ref>{{cite web|title=England hooker Josh Hodgson ruled out of World Cup final|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15314/11144922/england-hooker-josh-hodgson-ruled-out-of-world-cup-final|website=Sky Sports|access-date=20 January 2018|date=26 November 2017}}</ref>

In 2018 he was selected for [[England national rugby league team|England]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Leigh Sports Village]].<ref name="Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2018/10/17/tom-johnstone-marks-debut-england-hat-trick-against-france/|title=Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France|publisher=Guardian|date=17 October 2018|access-date=31 October 2019}}</ref>

He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|publisher=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref> He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test debut in the defeat by Tonga.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|publisher=Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>

== Post playing ==
On 26 July 2024 it was announced that Hodgson would take over the coaching role of the Parramatta Eels' Harold Matthews Cup squad in 2025.
